entities.name: śabda
entities.cite: Some who follow the ancient way say there is a difference of essence between the two

entities.name: sphota
entities.cite: the difference of what is in fact undivided

axioms.name: abhinna
axioms.cite: The eke, the others, hold that the sphota is abhinna, undivided, and that duality is buddhibheda-ja, born of a difference in the apprehending cognition

axioms.name: buddhibheda
axioms.cite: The eke, the others, hold that the sphota is abhinna, undivided, and that duality is buddhibheda-ja, born of a difference in the apprehending cognition

axioms.name: vivarta_compatible
axioms.cite: The second position is the one that coheres with the vivarta metaphysics of 1.1-2, where all plurality is appearance riding on the one

denials.name: atmabheda
denials.cite: The puranagas, followers of the old doctrine, hold atmabheda: sound and sphota are two in essence, really distinct items.

claims.name: buddhibheda
claims.cite: arises from a difference in cognition

rejected_readings.label: atmabheda_doctrine
rejected_readings.rendering: Sound and sphota are fundamentally different entities in essence.
rejected_readings.why: The commentary attributes atmabheda to puranagas but the endorsed position (buddhibheda) contradicts this. The verse makes clear that sphota is abhinna with only cognitive difference, not essential difference.

rejected_readings.label: sphota_manifestation
rejected_readings.rendering: Sphota is merely a manifestation or appearance of sound itself.
rejected_readings.why: The commentary emphasizes sphota as abhinna (undivided/independent), suggesting it transcends or equals sound ontologically, not as a derivative manifestation. Treating it as a manifestation demotes its status incorrectly.

rejected_readings.label: no_cognitive_variation
rejected_readings.rendering: Sound and sphota are identical without any cognitive difference whatsoever.
rejected_readings.why: The verse explicitly states that difference arises from cognitive variation (buddhibheda). Asserting identity without cognitive differentiation contradicts the buddhibheda relation.

notes: The verse presents two doctrinal positions (atmabheda vs buddhibheda) doxographically, but the commentary endorses buddhibheda as coherent with vivarta metaphysics. Axioms formalize the endorsed position: sphota is abhinna, yet appears different through cognitive difference (buddhibheda). The atmabheda view is attributed to puranagas and structurally denied.
